behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (request builder -> API client), not OS/network/model quality. An in-process fake client that captures outbound payload can catch missing/partial taxonomy labels. Rejected System because behavior text is about request contents, not real HTTP/network effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no correctness claim depends on model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake API transport that records the dispatched request. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert the outbound payload contains the full, exact label set every time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure can occur at response-handoff boundary (API response parsing -> normalization -> module return). A unit test of only normalization could miss wiring bugs that forward raw provider labels. In-process stubbed API response is sufficient; no real network/OS needed. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on invalid label mapping logic, not transport behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because model output quality is not under test; only guardrail handling is.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "medical"). Call the module end-to-end and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and the raw label is never surfaced.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets one local normalization rule with identical inputs and deterministic output; no module interaction is required. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text "normalization step" and "identical raw API responses," which isolates pure logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert output label is identical across runs and has no hidden state dependence.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text requires "repeated live API calls" and consistency of returned label, which depends on real model outputs (non-determinism/drift). Mocks would hide this failure mode by returning fixed labels. Rejected Workflow because this is a single model decision per call, not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real calls (same document, same taxonomy, same model/config) and track returned labels plus raw provider outputs. Assert label stability when provider outputs are unchanged; flag instability attributable to model response variation.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module policy enforcement (token-budget logic + dispatch gating). A fake transport can verify whether dispatch happened and with what payload length, catching wiring failures that pure unit tests can miss. Rejected System because behavior says "before dispatch," so real network/OS behavior is not required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Configure strict budget and policy, run classifier with oversized input, and use a fake API client to assert either truncated payload was sent within limit or request was rejected with no dispatch, per configured policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real external API behavior (provider tokenization/accounting); in-process fakes can always report compliant usage and miss real over-budget failures. Rejected Agentic because the assertion is on provider usage accounting, not model decision quality/content. Rejected Integration because behavior text depends on "provider-reported token usage," which a fake cannot faithfully reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real API call with a representative document and fixed model/settings; read provider-reported usage metadata and assert total tokens <= configured budget, failing if provider accounting exceeds limit.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests retry orchestration across local modules (error handling + backoff + retry call). A deterministic stub API (429 then success) with fake time/backoff can reproduce the failure without real network/OS primitives. Rejected System because real HTTP infrastructure is unnecessary for validating retry state transitions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model output decisions and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client scripted to return rate-limit error on first call and success on second. Assert module delays per backoff policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing the first error.
